what_it_does: A JSON registry that lists the project's surfaces (pages/views), design-system faces, documentation files, and proof commands, intended to be mounted by every surface so the whole estate is navigable from one source.
observations: The `meta` block declares this the one registry of the Design DNA estate and says every surface mounts the same Atlas chip.
`surfaces` lists pages/paths with human-readable names, descriptions, and sometimes `id` and `status` fields.
`faces` enumerates 19 conceptual design-system faces such as tokens, grammar, layouts, sequence, voice, motion, address, subject, reader, canon, and types.
`docs` lists 10 markdown documents plus a generated canon projection, each with a one-line summary.
`proofs` lists 8 node commands paired with terse coverage claims like 11/11, 7/7, and 10/10.
Some surface entries include `id` and `status`; the `viewer scaffold` entry lacks both.
Descriptions use interpretive, prose-style language with em-dashes and curly quotes rather than machine-actionable metadata.
standouts: Self-described as the single source of truth for the entire design DNA estate.
The faces list is large and explicitly treated as the system's conceptual vocabulary.
Proofs are paired with claimed pass ratios, suggesting a proof-driven workflow.
concerns: Schema inconsistency: some surfaces have `id`/`status`, others do not.
Paths are not normalized, mixing root-relative and file-relative forms.
Coverage ratios in proofs are asserted without detail in this file.
Descriptions are poetic rather than structured, limiting programmatic use.
No explicit schema or required-field list beyond the meta note.
notes: This file is the project's self-index: a hand-curated manifest of pages, concepts, docs, and checks that every surface is supposed to render.
questions: Where is `DNA.mountAtlas` implemented and how does each surface use it?
What determines whether a surface path is root-relative or a file path?
Are the 19 faces exhaustive, or can new faces be added freely?
What do the proof ratios (11/11, 7/7, 10/10) specifically count?
Is the `viewer scaffold` a surface, a component, or a primitive?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to be the one registry mounted by every surface; actual content is a curated manifest of surfaces, faces, docs, and proofs, which matches that purpose though it is not itself a routing or implementation file.
intention_signals: It tries to make the whole design system discoverable from one manifest, so no page or concept is hidden behind a filename and every surface stays in sync.
novelty: It treats a design system as a navigable estate of surfaces and faces rather than a flat component library, and explicitly links conceptual faces to documentation and automated proofs.
contribution: Provides a centralized, human-readable index that maps conceptual design-system faces, concrete surfaces, documentation, and verification commands into one addressable registry.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON manifest that registers the surfaces, design-system faces, documentation files, and proof commands of a design DNA estate.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as the single mounted registry that makes every page, concept, document, and check discoverable across all surfaces.
